questions.text: The property of a body to resist change in its state of rest or uniform motion is:
questions.explanation: Inertia is the inherent property of mass described in Newton's First Law.

questions.text: Newton's Second Law of Motion gives the measure of:
questions.explanation: F = dp/dt = ma.

questions.text: A rocket works on the principle of conservation of:
questions.explanation: Based on the ejection of gases providing upward thrust (Newton's 3rd Law).

questions.text: Static friction is:
questions.explanation: It increases with the applied force up to the limiting value.

questions.text: When a bus starts suddenly, passengers fall backwards. This is due to:
questions.explanation: The lower body moves with the bus while the upper body tends to stay at rest.

questions.text: The SI unit of impulse is:
questions.explanation: Impulse = Force × Time (N s).

questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg moves with acceleration 5 m/s². The net force is:
The coefficient of static friction is usually _____ than kinetic friction.
questions.explanation: It takes more force to start motion than to maintain it.

questions.text: Action and reaction forces act on:
questions.explanation: Newton's 3rd law pairs act on two interacting objects.

questions.text: Frictional force is a:
questions.explanation: It requires physical contact between surfaces.

questions.text: The maximum value of static friction is called:
questions.explanation: F_lim = μ_s N.

questions.text: A man is standing in a lift moving upwards with acceleration 'a'. His apparent weight is:
questions.explanation: Normal force N = mg + ma.

questions.text: If the net external force on a system is zero, the total momentum:
questions.explanation: Principle of Conservation of Linear Momentum.

questions.text: Which of the following is the weakest basic force in nature?
questions.explanation: Despite its dominance in space, gravity is the weakest at the particle level.

questions.text: The banking of roads is done to provide necessary:
questions.explanation: To allow cars to take turns at higher speeds safely.

questions.text: Impulse is equal to change in:
questions.explanation: Impulse-Momentum Theorem.

questions.text: A fielder pulls his hands back while catching a ball to:
options: Decrease rate of change of momentum
questions.explanation: Increasing time reduces the impact force (F = Δp/Δt).

questions.text: Angle of friction (θ) is related to coefficient of friction (μ) as:
questions.explanation: tan θ = f/N = μN/N = μ.

questions.text: Recoil of a gun is an example of:
questions.explanation: Action (bullet forward) and Reaction (gun backward).

questions.text: Why is it easier to pull a lawn mower than to push it?
questions.explanation: Pulling has a vertical component that opposes weight, reducing N and thus friction.

questions.text: Rolling friction is _____ than sliding friction.
questions.explanation: Reason why wheels were invented.

questions.text: Normal reaction is always _____ to the surface.
questions.explanation: 'Normal' means perpendicular.

questions.text: Which law is called the 'Real Law of Motion'?
questions.explanation: 1st and 3rd laws can be derived from the 2nd law.

questions.text: A body is in equilibrium if the net force is:
questions.explanation: Equilibrium means acceleration is zero, hence net force is zero.

questions.text: The direction of friction is always _____ to motion.
questions.explanation: Friction opposes relative motion or the tendency of motion.

questions.text: Lubrication is used to:
questions.explanation: Oil/Grease provides a smooth layer between surfaces.

questions.text: Tension in a string is a form of:
questions.explanation: Intermolecular forces within the string.

questions.text: A horse cannot pull a cart in empty space because there is no:
questions.explanation: Walking requires pushing the ground backward to get a forward reaction.

questions.text: Apparent weight of a man in a freely falling lift is:
questions.explanation: a = g, so N = m(g-g) = 0. Weightlessness.

questions.text: Force is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: It has both magnitude and direction.

questions.text: The unit of force, Newton, is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: From F = ma.

questions.text: Swimming is possible due to Newton's:
questions.explanation: Pushing water back (action) results in forward motion (reaction).

questions.text: Momentum (p) is defined as:
questions.explanation: The quantity of motion.

questions.text: Inertia of a body depends on its:
questions.explanation: Mass is the quantitative measure of inertia.

questions.text: If a body is moving on a frictionless horizontal surface with constant velocity, the force required to keep it moving is:
questions.explanation: Newton's 1st law: No net force is needed to maintain uniform motion.

questions.text: Centrifugal force is a:
questions.explanation: Observed only in non-inertial (rotating) frames of reference.

questions.text: Angle of repose is equal to:
questions.explanation: The angle at which an object just starts to slide down an inclined plane.

questions.text: Concurrent forces are those that:
questions.explanation: Their lines of action intersect at a single point.

questions.text: Friction can be reduced by using:
questions.explanation: Methods to reduce resistance to motion.

questions.text: If a force of 10N acts on a mass of 10kg, its acceleration is:
